<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534}}; {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634943]] Wikidata name: Kamo Wake-ikazuchi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634943]] Wikidata name: Kamo Wake-ikazuchi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto''' (賀茂別雷命) is a [[:en:Deity|Deity]] in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].
== Overview == Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is the {{ill|saijin|lt=deity enshrined|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} at [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]] (commonly known as Kamigamo Shrine) and is worshipped at various {{ill|Kamo shrines|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo shrines|qq=}} (賀茂神社 or 鴨神社) throughout Japan.
The deity does not appear in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. However, in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] text *Kamo no Honji* (賀茂之本地), Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is identified with {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|阿遅鉏高日子根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, a deity mentioned in the *Kojiki*.
An excerpt from the {{ill|Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Yamashiro Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a now-lost geographical and mythological record, recounts the following tale about Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi]], the deity of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Mioya Shrine]], had a daughter named {{ill|Tama Yori Hime|ja|玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Yori Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} (also enshrined at Kamo Mioya Shrine). While Tama Yori Hime was playing at the Seimi stream of the Ishikawa River (now known as the [[:en:Kamo River|Kamo River (Yodo River system)]]), a vermilion-painted arrow drifted down from upstream.
She brought the arrow home and placed it near her bed, which caused her to conceive a child. That child was Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. Upon reaching adulthood, during a celebration, his grandfather, Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi, said, "Offer this sake to the one you believe to be your father." At that moment, Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto ascended to the heavens, breaking through the roof, revealing that his father was a divine being. The vermilion-painted arrow was identified as the deity of {{ill|Mukō Shrine|lt=Mukō Shrine|lt=Hinoikazuchi Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (a fire and thunder deity).
=== Folklore === Similar stories of divine birth are found in the *Kojiki* (e.g., [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Himetataraisuzu-hime|Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime]]) and the *Hata Clan Genealogy* (秦氏本系帳, involving Arewotome and [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui]]). The latter story is often conflated with the tale of Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto, leading to the belief that his father is [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui]], the deity enshrined at [[:en:Matsunoo Taisha|Matsunoo Taisha]].
== See Also == * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534}}; {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}}}
'''Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto''' (賀茂別雷命) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=Deity}} in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}.
== Overview == Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is the {{ill|saijin|lt=deity enshrined|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} at {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}} (commonly known as Kamigamo Shrine) and is worshipped at various {{ill|Kamo shrines|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo shrines|qq=}} (賀茂神社 or 鴨神社) throughout Japan.
The deity does not appear in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}. However, in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} text *Kamo no Honji* (賀茂之本地), Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is identified with {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|阿遅鉏高日子根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, a deity mentioned in the *Kojiki*.
An excerpt from the {{ill|Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Yamashiro Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a now-lost geographical and mythological record, recounts the following tale about Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi}}, the deity of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine}}, had a daughter named {{ill|Tama Yori Hime|ja|玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Yori Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} (also enshrined at Kamo Mioya Shrine). While Tama Yori Hime was playing at the Seimi stream of the Ishikawa River (now known as the {{ill|Kamo River|en|Kamo River|WD=Q27218|lt=Kamo River (Yodo River system)}}), a vermilion-painted arrow drifted down from upstream.
She brought the arrow home and placed it near her bed, which caused her to conceive a child. That child was Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. Upon reaching adulthood, during a celebration, his grandfather, Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi, said, "Offer this sake to the one you believe to be your father." At that moment, Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto ascended to the heavens, breaking through the roof, revealing that his father was a divine being. The vermilion-painted arrow was identified as the deity of {{ill|Mukō Shrine|lt=Mukō Shrine|lt=Hinoikazuchi Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (a fire and thunder deity).
=== Folklore === Similar stories of divine birth are found in the *Kojiki* (e.g., {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|en|Himetataraisuzu-hime|WD=Q5353793|lt=Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime}}) and the *Hata Clan Genealogy* (秦氏本系帳, involving Arewotome and {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui}}). The latter story is often conflated with the tale of Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto, leading to the belief that his father is {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui}}, the deity enshrined at {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|en|Matsunoo Taisha|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsunoo Taisha}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}}

== See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[ja:賀茂別雷命]][[simple:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi]][[zh:賀茂別雷命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|parents={{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534}}; {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}}}
'''Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto''' (賀茂別雷命) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=Deity}} in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}.
== Overview == Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is the {{ill|saijin|lt=deity enshrined|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} at {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}} (commonly known as Kamigamo Shrine) and is worshipped at various {{ill|Kamo shrines|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo shrines|qq=}} (賀茂神社 or 鴨神社) throughout Japan.
The deity does not appear in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}. However, in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} text *Kamo no Honji* (賀茂之本地), Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is identified with {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|阿遅鉏高日子根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, a deity mentioned in the *Kojiki*.
An excerpt from the {{ill|Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Yamashiro Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a now-lost geographical and mythological record, recounts the following tale about Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi}}, the deity of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine}}, had a daughter named {{ill|Tama Yori Hime|ja|玉依姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Yori Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} (also enshrined at Kamo Mioya Shrine). While Tama Yori Hime was playing at the Seimi stream of the Ishikawa River (now known as the {{ill|Kamo River|en|Kamo River|WD=Q27218|lt=Kamo River (Yodo River system)}}), a vermilion-painted arrow drifted down from upstream.
She brought the arrow home and placed it near her bed, which caused her to conceive a child. That child was Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. Upon reaching adulthood, during a celebration, his grandfather, Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi, said, "Offer this sake to the one you believe to be your father." At that moment, Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto ascended to the heavens, breaking through the roof, revealing that his father was a divine being. The vermilion-painted arrow was identified as the deity of {{ill|Mukō Shrine|lt=Mukō Shrine|lt=Hinoikazuchi Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (a fire and thunder deity).
=== Folklore === Similar stories of divine birth are found in the *Kojiki* (e.g., {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|en|Himetataraisuzu-hime|WD=Q5353793|lt=Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime}}) and the *Hata Clan Genealogy* (秦氏本系帳, involving Arewotome and {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui}}). The latter story is often conflated with the tale of Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto, leading to the belief that his father is {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui}}, the deity enshrined at {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|en|Matsunoo Taisha|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsunoo Taisha}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}}

While Tama Yori Hime was playing at the Seimi stream of the Ishikawa River (now known as the {{ill|Kamo River|en|Kamo River|WD=Q27218|lt=Kamo River (Yodo River system)|1=Kamo River}}), a vermilion-painted arrow drifted down from upstream.
She brought the arrow home and placed it near her bed, which caused her to conceive a child. That child was Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. Upon reaching adulthood, during a celebration, his grandfather, Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi, said, "Offer this sake to the one you believe to be your father." At that moment, Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto ascended to the heavens, breaking through the roof, revealing that his father was a divine being. The vermilion-painted arrow was identified as the deity of {{ill|Mukō Shrine|lt=Mukō Shrine|lt=Hinoikazuchi Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (a fire and thunder deity).
=== Folklore === Similar stories of divine birth are found in the *Kojiki* (e.g., {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Himetataraisuzu-hime|en|Himetataraisuzu-hime|WD=Q5353793|lt=Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}}) and the *Hata Clan Genealogy* (秦氏本系帳, involving Arewotome and {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}). The latter story is often conflated with the tale of Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto, leading to the belief that his father is {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}, the deity enshrined at {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|en|Matsunoo Taisha|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsunoo Taisha|1=Matsunoo Taisha}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}}

'''Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto''' (賀茂別雷命) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=Deity|1=Deity}} in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}.
== Overview == Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is the {{ill|saijin|lt=deity enshrined|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} at {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (commonly known as Kamigamo Shrine) and is worshipped at various {{ill|Kamo shrines|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo shrines|qq=}} (賀茂神社 or 鴨神社) throughout Japan.
The deity does not appear in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. However, in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} text *Kamo no Honji* (賀茂之本地), Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto is identified with {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|阿遅鉏高日子根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, a deity mentioned in the *Kojiki*.

While Tama Yori Hime was playing at the Seimi stream of the Ishikawa River (now known as the {{ill|Kamo River|en|Kamo River|WD=Q27218|lt=Kamo River (Yodo River system)|1=Kamo River}}), a vermilion-painted arrow drifted down from upstream.
She brought the arrow home and placed it near her bed, which caused her to conceive a child. That child was Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto. Upon reaching adulthood, during a celebration, his grandfather, Kamo no Tachi no Tsunomi, said, "Offer this sake to the one you believe to be your father." At that moment, Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto ascended to the heavens, breaking through the roof, revealing that his father was a divine being. The vermilion-painted arrow was identified as the deity of {{ill|Mukō Shrine|lt=Mukō Shrine|lt=Hinoikazuchi Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (a fire and thunder deity).

The latter story is often conflated with the tale of Kamo Wake Ikazuchi no Mikoto, leading to the belief that his father is {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}, the deity enshrined at {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|en|Matsunoo Taisha|WD=Q710302|lt=Matsunoo Taisha|1=Matsunoo Taisha}}.
